figures.caption: Table 3. Classification performance for the MIMIC-IV dataset across different tasks under varying missing-data configurations.

Figure 1. Overview of the proposed CondI framework. (a) Global Federated Workflow. The server coordinates the distribution and aggregation of global model parameters Θ and prompt embeddings W across clients via FedAvg. (b) Local Two-Phase Pipeline & Inference. Inside each client, the process is structurally decoupled. In Phase A, the missing pattern (Wcond) is extracted to explicitly guide the diffusion model in imputing missing data. In Phase B, the model optimizes the modality profile (Wmod) and trains the instance encoder. During the inference stage (dotted line), the explicitly imputed data is fed back into the encoder to extract refined instance features (Wins), which are then concatenated with Wmod and Wcond for downstream task prediction.
